meta.title: How to Fine-tune Florence 2: The Best Small Vision Model
meta.description:
➡️ Florence 2 Colab Notebook: https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1t0C7pYtcrS_BOR-0jHVL8QsZsfMNtTih?usp=sharing
➡️ Get Life-time Access to the Complete Scripts (and future improvements): https://Trelis.com/ADVANCED-vision/
➡️ One-click fine-tuning and LLM templates: https://github.com/TrelisResearch/one-click-llms
➡️ Trelis Newsletter: https://blog.Trelis.com
➡️ Resources/Support/Discord: https://Trelis.com/About
➡️ Thumbnail made with this tutorial: https://youtu.be/ThKYjTdkyP8

VIDEO RESOURCES:
Slides: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1djNIaMCcDRBuMj-GPX5TClMn03gZjMpsArixedR_MCo/edit?usp=sharing
- Chess Dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Trelis/chess_pieces
- Chess Dataset w/ bounding boxes: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Trelis/chess_bounding_boxes
- Florence paper: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2311.06242
- Florence base model on HF: https://huggingface.co/microsoft/Florence-2-base-ft
- Thumbnail created using Flux Schnell in this video: https://youtu.be/ThKYjTdkyP8

TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 Object and Bounding Box Detection with Vision Models
0:42 Video Overview
2:15 Florence 2 Architecture
8:03 How bounding boxes and segmentation work
12:55 Inferencing Florence 2 in Google Colab. Bounding boxes and segmentation.
21:17 Annotating a custom dataset with bounding boxes.
28:06 Fine-tuning Florence 2 on bounding boxes. Notebook demo.
41:41 Setting up LoRA adapters
44:12 How to choose learning rate and training arguments
48:40 Fine-tuning results and performance
49:43 Video Resources
